title: The First Awakening
description: The king's golem has been tested with five ingredients. Use the reaction results and mixing records to determine which two ingredients will animate it.

hints.text:
The golem’s chest and ears each react to ONE specific color+size property — you must figure out which from the test results and mixing records. The bottom grid below is where you work this out.

Reaction test results:
  ing1: chest ✓ ears ✓  →  reacts to both
  ing2: chest ✗ ears ✓  →  ears-only
  ing3: chest ✓ ears ✓  →  reacts to both
  ing4: chest ✗ ears ✓  →  ears-only
  ing7: chest ✗ ears ✗  →  non-reactive

Start by looking at mix(ing3, ing4) = R+. What does that tell you about the R-channel of ing3 and ing4?

mix(ing3, ing4) = R+ → both have R-sign=‘+’ and opposite R-sizes (one R Large, one R Small).
  Chest reacts to ing3 but NOT ing4 → their R-size differs → chest = R Large or R Small.
  Ears reacts to BOTH → they share the same size in the ears’ color. In an R+ mix the G- and B-channels are neutral (signs cancel), so both have the same G-size. → ears = G Large or G Small.

Pin the G-size: mix(ing4, ing7) = G-. ing4 reacts to ears, ing7 does not → they differ in G-size → ing4 has G Small, ing7 has G Large → ears reacts to G Small.

Pin the R-size: try chest = R Large. Then ing3 has R Large and ing4 has R Small → chest reacts to ing3 only ✓. The only R+ pair with one R+Large and one R+Small is Ppn + pnP → ing3 = Ppn, ing4 = pnP, chest = R Large ✓.

Mark the bottom grid: Chest → R Large, Ears → G Small.

Animation is SIGN-based (not size-based): a Large-reacting part implies animSign=‘+’; Small → animSign=‘−’.
Chest = R Large → animators need R+. Ears = G Small → animators need G−. Both conditions must hold.

Step 1 — mix(ing3, ing4) = R+: ing3 = Ppn, ing4 = pnP.
  pnP: R+ ✓, G− ✓ → ANIMATOR.

Step 2 — mix(ing2, ing3) = B-: mix(npN, Ppn) = B-. → ing2 = npN (R− ✗, not an animator).

Step 3 — mix(ing4, ing7) = G-: mix(pnP, pNn) = G-. → ing7 = pNn.
  pNn: R+ ✓, G− ✓ → ANIMATOR.

The animators are ing4 (pnP) and ing7 (pNn).
